title: This month in KDE Linux: March 2026
tags: KDE Linux;the last one;https://kde.org/linux;weren’t as atomic as intended;fairly serious bug;Our custom system for deploying changes to /etc;documented set of recovery steps;fixed the bug on our side;automatically roll back and forward /etc as you roll back and forward OS builds;adapted Discover’s sysupdate support;https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/41303;https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/41299;https://github.com/systemd/systemd/issues/41288;broke many TPM;tweaked the parameters of our zram setup;fixed our “out of memory” handler;deep research into memory management;integrated a component;displays sane feedback;pre-installed usbmuxd;fixed an issue;slightly increased the amount of time that the OS image chooser is visible on screen;added support for mounting optical disk images;support for SSTP VPNs;and also;display translated text;screen locking;update checking;disabled canceling the installation in the middle;display a little “*” for every character you type;to preserve metadata;fixed our spellchecking setup;set the $SSH_ASKPASS environment;KSSHAskPass;install KDE Linux;report issues;KDE Linux’s documentation;here;packaging or code issues in Flatpak-packaged apps;Beta milestone;High priority tasks for technical experts;Other tasks to make the user builds better;Tasks for newcomers
